code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple template map for more natural phrasing
    template_map = {
        "location": "is located in {}",
        "cityServed": "serves {}",
        "country": "is in {}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of {} meters above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has an elevation of {} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has an elevation of {} metres above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {} meters",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface of {}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has first runway number {}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has a second runway surface of {}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has a third runway surface of {}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of {}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fifth runway surface of {}",
        "leader": "has leader {}",
        "capital": "has capital {}",
        "language": "uses language {}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {}",
        "currency": "uses currency {}",
        "owner": "is owned by {}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {}",
        "founder": "was founded by {}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {}",
        "leaderParty": "has leader party {}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {}",
        "chief": "has chief {}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {}",
        "nativeName": "has native name {}",
        "class": "belongs to class {}",
        "division": "belongs to division {}",
        "order": "belongs to order {}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {}",
    }

    # Group clauses by subject
    subject_clauses = {}
    for t in triples:
        obj = t.object.strip('"')
        tmpl = template_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(obj)
        else:
            # Fallback: simple “has <predicate> <object>”
            pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
            clause = f"has {pred} {obj}"
        subject_clauses.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(clause)

    sentences = []
    for subj, parts in subject_clauses.items():
        if not parts:
            continue
        if len(parts) == 1:
            sentence = f"{subj} {parts[0]}."
        else:
            # join with commas and an 'and' before the last part
            body = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
            sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences for different subjects
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
